title: Jehovah Nissi: The Lord Our Banner of Victory
summary:
The name Jehovah Nissi, meaning “The Lord Our Banner,” reveals God as our source of strength and victory. This sermon explores its significance through three key narratives: Moses' intercession, Jesus' role as the ensign of nations, and the resurrection as a declaration of triumph.

In Exodus 17:8-13, Moses leads Israel in battle against Amalek. As Moses raises the staff in prayer, Israel prevails; when he tires, they falter. With the support of Aaron and Hur, his hands remain steady, symbolizing the power of prayer, reliance on God, and the importance of community in spiritual battles. Just as Moses depended on others to sustain him, we too need the support of fellow believers to endure life’s challenges.

Jesus fulfills the prophetic vision in Isaiah 11:10 as the "ensign" for all nations. His life, sacrifice, and resurrection serve as a universal call to salvation, extending hope and redemption beyond cultural and historical boundaries. His death on the cross, declared by His words, “It is finished” (John 19:30), marks the ultimate victory over sin and death, offering freedom to all who believe.

The resurrection solidifies our identity as “more than conquerors” (Romans 8:37). When Jesus appeared to the disciples (John 20:19-20), He dispelled their fear and renewed their hope. His wounds testify to His sacrifice, reminding us that He understands our struggles and stands as our banner of victory.

As followers of Christ, we are called to reflect His light, live with unity and courage, and lift others who are weary. Every act of love, kindness, and service becomes an opportunity to elevate His banner and invite others into His hope. Under Jehovah Nissi, we stand victorious, empowered to share His message with a world in need.
